rows.indicator: yellow and white and green road markings
rows.description: New Zealand uses a yellow divider line and white shoulder lines. There are some cases where you can find just white dashed lines. This means that you can pass other drivers. You can also find these in cities. They tell you that there is no parking in this spot. You can also find Green bus lanes and red or green bicycle lanes in urban areas.
New Zealand uses English and Māori. Most of the North Island contains both languages. A key way to know if a place is a Māori word is if it ends in common vowel pairs, like ‘ui’, ‘ea’, or ‘oa’.

(Front). (Back). New Zealand has some unique bollards, so it is worth learning them. If you don’t, you may often confused New Zealand with Australia. (Front). (Back). You can also find these near bridges.

rows.indicator: Chevron in New Zealand is yellow and black
rows.description: The Chevron in New Zealand is yellow and black, however it has this unique thin black border around the edge.
Some cities will have some taller skyscrapers, but they’re not too common of a sight. New Zealand has quite a few houses with this metal roof. Some are in better condition, while others are rusty.
These concrete poles are mostly found in Suburbs and urban areas. The wooden poles are mostly found in rural areas, but they can also be found closer to urban areas.

rows.indicator: New Zealand uses a solid yellow line for its stop line
rows.description: New Zealand uses a solid yellow line for its stop line.

rows.indicator: They just make the assumption that the drivers know better
rows.description: They just make the assumption that the drivers know better.

New Zealand has these green signs that are on most major roads. That red shield is important as it is the New Zealand State road system. New Zealand uses Diagonal warning signs, and they appear on White wooden poles. The exception is pedestrian crossings, which have black and white striped poles. New Zealand’s Yield signs are white and red. They also have a white painted triangle on the road. This is a unique combination.

Area codes in New Zealand can be incredibly useful. 02 = Cellphone. 03 = Entire South Island. 04 = Wellington area. 06 = East side of North Island. 07 = Central and west side of North Island. 09 = North end of North Island (from Auckland northwards).

This is the General feel of the North Island. While New Zealand is really diverse, its a generic feel. This is the General feel of the South Island. The best thing to do is imagine the Lord of the Rings movies. If it looks like Middle Earth, its New Zealand.

rows.indicator: These metal poles are found mostly on large highways and near some major cities
rows.description: These metal poles are found mostly on large highways and near some major cities. New Zealand uses Yellow poles for their traffic lights. There were found in major cities quite frequently.

rows.indicator: New Zealand’s speed limit signs do not have a KM/H under the number
rows.description: New Zealand’s speed limit signs do not have a KM/H under the number.

warnings: Local deterministic extraction used GeoTips text only; associated images were not interpreted, so descriptions contain no image-derived details.
